description: To make physical contact with something. 'Palo' names the most intimate of senses, requiring proximity and usually intention; it covers the action of touching and the sensation of being touched. Touch confirms reality, offers comfort, and communicates what words cannot.
sound_symbolism: The word is brief and direct, like touch itself—immediate contact. 'Pa' begins with the bilabial stop, lips meeting as surfaces meet in touch. The 'a' opens at the moment of contact. Then 'lo' flows gently, the sustained sensation after first contact, pressure becoming information.
grammatical_notes: 'mia thena palo' (I touch a thing). The event noun appears by rule; in a copular sentence: 'palo welao nai.' (touch is good). Can specify instrument with 'roe': 'mia roe manuwe thena palo' (I touch a thing with the hand).

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Touch communicates care, comfort, and connection beyond words. Consensual touch builds trust and community.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Hands learn through touch—the craftsperson knows materials by feel, the healer reads the body through contact.
